topic: interpreting box-and-whisker plots, quartiles, and interquartile range
latex:
The box-and-whisker plot represents the numbers of cocoons in each butterfly tent.

\begin{tikzpicture}
% Number line
\draw[<->] (-0.5,0) -- (9.5,0);
\foreach \x/\label in {0/0, 1/2, 2/4, 3/6, 4/8, 5/10, 6/12, 7/14, 8/16, 9/18} {
  \draw (\x,0.1) -- (\x,-0.1) node[below] {\label};
}
% Whiskers
\draw (1,0.5) -- (4,0.5); % left whisker from 2 to 8
\draw (7,0.5) -- (8,0.5); % right whisker from 14 to 16
% Box
\draw (4,0.3) rectangle (7,0.7); % box from 8 to 14
% Median line
\draw (5.5,0.3) -- (5.5,0.7); % median at 11
% Endpoints
\fill (1,0.5) circle (2pt); % min at 2
\fill (8,0.5) circle (2pt); % max at 16
\fill (4,0.5) circle (2pt); % Q1 at 8
\fill (5.5,0.5) circle (2pt); % median at 11
\fill (7,0.5) circle (2pt); % Q3 at 14
\end{tikzpicture}

\textbf{a.} What percent of the butterfly tents contain at most 10 cocoons?

\textbf{b.} Are the data more spread out below the first quartile or above the third quartile? Explain.

\textbf{c.} Find and interpret the interquartile range of the data.
